mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate keeps the equity discount rate compressed, systematically supporting higher forward equity valuations and producing a positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the low-rate regime persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of latest snapshot, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60-day SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX shows no significant positive drift (t-test p>0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate persistently above 7% signals labor market slack consistent with a still-fragile post-recession recovery, raising downside risk to consumer spending and corporate earnings, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month until the rate meaningfully declines.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances (UNRATE>7%) across the sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) jointly confirms a broad-based economic expansion that neither series alone reliably signals; this joint confirmation predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, whereas divergence between the two series produces no reliable signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=133009K and INDPRO=97.29 in latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint-positive PAYEMS/INDPRO signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week whenever retail sales are expanding.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) occurring simultaneously with a rising federal funds rate indicates that monetary tightening is actively suppressing rate-sensitive demand, foreshadowing broader economic slowdown and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; the effect is absent when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered joint instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed either single-series signal's Rank IC by a meaningful margin over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side output growth, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend and forward 20-day SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or INDPRO uptrends coincide with negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month rise in the federal funds rate (>0.25pp) signals an abrupt shift toward monetary tightening that raises discount rates and increases near-term equity risk premia, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPI (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth above 2.5% raises market expectations of future Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month until inflation expectations stabilize.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
